---
title: Dockerfiler
abstract: A Python package that generates Dockerfiles given some prompts
categories: Personal project
image: "/imgs/dockerfiler.png"
author: 
    - name: Naïm Saadi Gallego
      url: https://linkedin.com/in/nsaadig16
---

<img src="/imgs/dockerfiler.png" width=300 align=center class="imgs">

<p></p>

## Description

**Dockerfiler** is a Python package that creates Dockerfiles based on templates and parameters entered by the user.

It uses Jinja2 for the templates and InquirerPy to retrieve the specific parameters.

Check the repo [here](https://github.com/nsaadig16/dockerfiler)! 

The package is published to [PyPi](https://pypi.org/project/dockerfiler/) and can be used by running  `uvx dockerfiler`.

## Development process

<p align="justify">
I was tired of making the same Dockerfile again and again, so I decided to develop a tool that could automate the procedure.
I started looking into *Jinja2* for the creation of templates, and decided to publish the package
in PyPi so that me and everyone who needed it could use it in a very easy way.

It relies heavily on the *extension* of previously created templates, so that the addition of new ones is fast and simple.
</p>

## Technologies used

![Python](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/devicons/devicon@latest/icons/python/python-original-wordmark.svg){width=60}
![Docker](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/devicons/devicon@latest/icons/docker/docker-plain-wordmark.svg){width=60}
![PyPi](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/devicons/devicon@latest/icons/pypi/pypi-original-wordmark.svg){width=60}
